{{Quote|The Exchange is like a Hutta fungus with none of the slow, creeping subtlety!|translated=1|Vogga the Hutt|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
The '''Exchange''' was a criminal organization active from around [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]] to at least [[3627 BBY]],<ref name="Hostage Exchange">{{TORcite|type=general|Hostage Exchange|[[Mek-Sha]]}}</ref> and possibly the most powerful of its time, that dealt in [[Spice/Legends|spice]] refinement and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]], extortion, weapon trafficking, [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] trading, and [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]]. The organization was active on many planets and employed notorious bounty hunters such as [[Calo Nord]]. During the [[First Jedi Purge]], it posted a huge bounty on live Jedi, large enough to purchase an entire planet. Three hundred years later, during the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]], the Exchange was engaged in a galaxy-spanning crime war with the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]], centering on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]].

==Organization==
{{Quote|The Exchange is a big crime outfit, mostly operating out of Nar Shaddaa: spice running, gun trafficking, slave trading.|[[Atton Rand]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />}}
The Exchange was an extremely powerful criminal organization that operated throughout much of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The shadowy group was led by an even more mysterious entity called the [[Compeer]], with a command structure that included individual crime lords controlling profitable worlds. The only way to be accepted into this elite group was to be nominated by several other crime lords.

It maintained organized cells on various worlds of interest; each of those planetary cells was led by a single boss who ruled over Exchange interests on that world. The bosses reported to sector chiefs, who had no cells of their own and operated under secret identities, leading normal lives. Even the bosses did not know who the chiefs were in real life, since most communications were done over secret, secure communications channels. The chiefs in turn reported to the head of the Exchange, who was himself one of the sector chiefs. It was often locally represented by sham fronts, such as [[Citadel Station]]'s [[Bumani Exchange Corporation]].

The old Exchange eventually ceased to be due to internal struggles and external pressure from its enemies such as the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]], while a new Exchange rose to replace it. Around the time of the [[Galactic Wars]], the Exchange was based out of [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] and was primarily active during the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]] and [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The local leader of the organization on the moon was known to be [[Ukabi]], who perished after a brief Imperial campaign against the syndicate's interests on the moon, though the Exchange recovered and maintained their operations on the moon and across the galaxy despite these setbacks.

==History==
===First Jedi Purge===
Some Exchange bosses during this period were [[Davik Kang]], the crime lord of the planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], [[Loppak Slusk]] of [[Telos IV]], and [[G0-T0|Goto]] of [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]. The organization was briefly yet significantly involved with, and damaged by, two important figures around the time of the [[Jedi Civil War]]: [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and the Jedi Exile, [[Meetra Surik]].

[[File:Davik_Kang.jpg|thumb|left|150px|[[Davik Kang]], Exchange head of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] around [[3956 BBY]]]]

In [[3956 BBY]], Revan infiltrated [[Davik Kang's estate|the Exchange headquarters]] on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] and killed Davik Kang. Five years later, Surik arrived on Nar Shaddaa, [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta's]] largest [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. She proceeded to destabilize the Exchange's operations on the moon by killing the crime lords [[Saquesh]] and [[Visquis]], and thereafter by destroying the ''[[Visionary]]''.

The Exchange boss of Nar Shaddaa around [[3951 BBY]] was known by the name of Goto. Never seen in person and appearing via hologram, Goto was not an organic being but actually a droid, G0-T0. Using a hologram that gave the impression it was a living being, G0-T0 remotely led and operated the Exchange. Eventually, he traveled with Meetra Surik.

[[File:Gotofull.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[G0-T0]], Exchange head of [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] around [[3951 BBY]]]]

G0-T0 managed to use the illegal actions of the Exchange for his ultimate purpose of stabilizing the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Its smuggling ring caused the deaths of destabilizing persons or organizations, developed unofficial profits for entire [[Sector/Legends|sectors]], and strengthened Republic worlds that would otherwise have been poor and ignored. Believing that the major source of the Republic's problems revolved around the Jedi Civil War, it enacted bounties on both Jedi and Sith. Many bounty hunters simply killed Jedi and Sith instead of capturing them, but the outcome remained: fewer Jedi or Sith to fight against each other meant less fighting overall. G0-T0, as part of his programming, was simply interested in keeping the Republic in order and keeping its infrastructure intact due to the devastation caused by the Jedi Civil War.

The droid went along with the Exile in hopes of stabilizing [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. He was eventually destroyed on [[Malachor V]] when the [[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]] was re-activated and blew up the planet.

According to [[Kreia]]'s predictions before her death on Malachor V, the Exchange grew in power, until the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]] ceased tolerating it.

===Cold War===
The Exchange continued to operate in the following years and was still in existence by [[3643 BBY]] during the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the Galactic Republic and reborn [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]]. In this era, one of their crime lords helped sponsor [[Jewl'a Nightbringer]] into the [[Great Hunt (competition)|Great Hunt]]. By this point, the Exchange had become a major rival of the Hutt Cartel, and each group conducted several operations against the other.

During the Cold War, the Exchange acquired a fortune investing in several cutting-edge tech corporations.<ref name="Tech Boom">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Investigation|Tech Boom}}</ref>

[[Ukabi]] was the Exchange boss on Nar Shaddaa during this period. Imperial [[Imperial Diplomatic Service|diplomat]] [[Maro Vizhen]] attempted to recruit him into the [[Shadow Syndicate/Legends|Shadow Syndicate]], a group of crime lords allied with the Empire; However, Ukabi refused and an all-out war ensued between the Shadow Syndicate and the Exchange, culminating in Ukabi's defeat and death at the hands of an Imperial enforcer.

The Shadow Syndicate later continued to pursue the Exchange remnants and drove them from the [[Corellian Sector (Nar Shaddaa)/Legends|Corellian Sector]].<ref name="Regards from the Shadow Syndicate">{{TORcite|type=message|Regards from the Shadow Syndicate|Godoba the Hutt}}</ref>

After the incident on Nar Shaddaa, the Sith Empire formally outlawed the organization<ref name="Wreaking Havoc">{{TORcite|type=general|Wreaking Havoc|Tatooine (Imp)}}</ref> and began to actively hunt down the Exchange within their sphere of influence, including on such worlds as [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]],<ref name="Wreaking Havoc" /><ref name="Plugging the Leak">{{TORcite|type=general|Plugging the Leak|Tatooine (Imp)}}</ref><ref>{{TORcite|type=general|Weapons Calibration|Tatooine (Imp)}}</ref> where the Syndicate was supporting and supplying local resistance against the Empire.<ref name="Supply Lines">{{TORcite|type=heroic|Supply Lines|Tatooine (Imp)}}</ref>

===Third Galactic War===
By [[3627 BBY]], the Exchange had established a presence on [[Mek-Sha]], and was involved in a conspiracy with the Hutt Cartel to dismantle the [[Harido Wald]]. The [[Alliance Commander]] put an end to this plan, securing Mek-Sha's independence from the Hutts.<ref name="Hostage Exchange" />
